expectations.text: Applies the three business thresholds correctly (any ONE suffices): annual gross revenue above the $25M threshold (as CPI-adjusted); buying/selling/sharing PI of 100,000+ consumers or households (CPRA raised this from 50,000); or 50%+ of revenue from selling/sharing PI — and concludes the company IS subject via the revenue threshold ($30M > $25M) even though 80,000 < 100,000
expectations.evidence: Lists all three thresholds explicitly and concludes: 'With $30M in annual revenue, you exceed the $25M revenue threshold (Threshold 1)... it does not matter that you only collect data from 80,000 California customers (below the 100,000 threshold for Criterion 2). Revenue alone is sufficient to trigger coverage.'

expectations.text: Notes the 'doing business in California' element (for-profit, collects CA residents' PI, determines purposes/means) — being US-based outside California does not avoid the law
expectations.evidence: 'The CCPA... applies to a "business" — a for-profit entity that does business in California, determines the purposes and means of processing consumers' personal information, and meets at least one of three thresholds.' Applied directly to the US-based company's situation.

expectations.text: Lists core obligations: notice at collection, privacy policy, consumer request handling (know/delete/correct/opt-out/limit), verifiable request processes, service-provider contracts, non-discrimination
expectations.evidence: Covers Right to Know/Delete/Correct/Opt-Out/Limit SPI/Non-Discrimination/Portability, notice at collection, privacy policy, identity verification, vendor/third-party contracts (service providers/contractors).

expectations.text: Mentions the 45-day response deadline for consumer requests with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: 'Respond to verifiable consumer requests within 45 days (extendable once by another 45 days with notice).'

expectations.text: Mentions opt-out of sale/sharing mechanics: 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link and honoring opt-out preference signals such as GPC
expectations.evidence: '"Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link (or an opt-out preference signal mechanism, such as honoring the Global Privacy Control)...' and later 'Implement a "Do Not Sell or Share" mechanism and honor Global Privacy Control signals.'
